docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Discord is a free communication platform that allows users to send messages, share files, and host voice and video calls. This guide provides step-by-step instructions for setting up and using Discord for team collaboration in the CST program.

For more information about Discord's full range of features, visit the Discord website.

docs.title: Is This Guide for You?
docs.text: This guide is written for term 1 CST students who have no prior experience with Discord. The instructions work on any operating system, including macOS and Windows.

docs.title: By the End of This Guide, You Will Be Able To
docs.text:
1. Download, install, and set up a Discord account
  2. Create and configure a Discord server
  3. Create channels and manage permissions in a Discord server
  4. Host a team meeting using Discord

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, make sure you have the following:

  • A computer running macOS or Windows
  • A valid email address
  • A stable internet connection
  • Basic familiarity with mouse and keyboard operations

docs.title: Typographical Conventions
docs.text:
This guide uses the following typographic conventions:

Convention Description Example Commands & Actions Bolded verbs indicate actions you must perform Click, Type, Select, Press [Menu & Button Sequence] Square brackets indicate menus or buttons. The → symbol shows the order of clicks [User Settings] → [Voice & Video] → [Save Changes] Keyboard Keys Monospace text indicates keys to press Enter, Ctrl, Shift

docs.title: Admonitions
docs.text:
This guide uses three types of messages to provide additional information:

Success

A Success message describes the expected outcome of a completed step.

Warning

A Warning message highlights an action that may cause an error or unexpected result.

Note

A Note provides helpful context or extra detail for a step.

docs.title: Task 2: Create and Configure a Discord Server
Introduction
docs.text:
This section explains how to create and configure a Discord server for team collaboration using essential server-level settings.

For more information about Discord features, visit the Discord website.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• You have a Discord account and are signed in.
  • You are using Discord desktop app or web version.
  • You know the purpose of your server (for example, term project team collaboration).

Note

Discord desktop is recommended because server settings are easier to navigate.

docs.title: Setup Steps
docs.text:
1. Open Discord and click + (Add a Server).

  2. Select Create My Own and choose the server type. Why: This creates a custom server suited to your team’s use case.

  3. Choose the server purpose, if not sure. can skip this question.

  4. Enter a clear server name and click Create. Why: A clear name helps teammates identify the correct server quickly.

  5. Open the server dropdown and click Server Settings. Why: All core configuration options are managed in Server Settings.

  6. In Overview, confirm server name and upload a server icon, then save. Why: Basic identity settings make the server recognizable and professional.

  7. In Enable Community, complete setup (recommended). Why: Community setup enables useful management and baseline safety features.

  8. In Safety Setup/Moderation, set Verification Level to at least Medium. Why: Verification helps prevent spam or fake accounts from posting immediately.

  9. Enable available content/media safety filters and save. Why: These filters reduce inappropriate content and keep collaboration space clean.

  10. Set default notifications to Only @mentions. Why: This reduces notification overload while preserving important alerts.

  11. Configure invite link expiration and max uses in Invite People → Edit Invite Link. Why: Restricted invite links improve access control and reduce unauthorized sharing.

Success

Core server setup is complete and ready for your next tasks.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: Your Discord server is now created and configured with essential identity, safety, notification, and invite settings. This provides a strong foundation for the next implementation steps in your project workflow.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Discord is a communications platform that enables you to communicate with lecturer and classmates during your time in BCIT. This guide explains how to create an account, add friends and start your first conversation.

docs.title: Create An Account
docs.text:
To use Discord, you must first create an account. This section will show you how to register a free Discord account.

  1. Click "Register" to create a new account.

  2. Enter your email address, username, password, date of birth and click "Create Account" button.

  3. Account created and direct to the main page. You can verify your email now or later by clicking link your received in you mail box.

docs.title: Add Friend
docs.text: After creating an account, you can add your classmates to start a conversation with them. This section will show you how to add friend in Discord.

docs.title: Option 1: Send Request
docs.text:
1. Click "Friends" button direct to friends manage page.

  2. Enter the username of your friends and click "Send Friend Request" button.

  3. Click "Pending" button to view all the pending request you send.

  4. Click "All" button to view list of your friend.

docs.title: Start Chat
docs.text:
1. Choose the friend you want to communicate with and click it.

  2. Enter your message and send it.
